title: Managed Identity Authentication Story
summary: Microsoft recommends Microsoft Entra authentication for new Azure DevOps integrations.
analogy: Managed identity is a military ID badge minted by headquarters just in time. It is not a photocopied key. The door still opens only if the base security office added that badge to the access roster.
diagram:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Host as Azure host
    participant Entra as Microsoft Entra
    participant ADO as Azure DevOps
    Host->>Entra: Request ADO access token
    Entra-->>Host: Short lived bearer token
    Host->>ADO: API call with token
    ADO-->>Host: Authorized result
body:
## First principles

First principles: the safest automation identity is not a password. It is a workload identity issued by the cloud, granted only the permissions it needs, and represented as short lived access tokens.

## Definition

Microsoft recommends Microsoft Entra authentication for new Azure DevOps integrations. For Azure hosted workloads, a managed identity can request an Azure DevOps access token using the Azure DevOps audience or scope, including 499b84ac-1321-427f-aa17-267ca6975798/.default or https://app.vssps.visualstudio.com/.default. That identity must also be explicitly added to the Azure DevOps organization and granted permissions.

## How it fits together

Do not call a PAT managed identity. If the official MCP path you use only accepts interactive OAuth, Azure CLI, or PAT, treat PAT as a gap or temporary anti-pattern. The target pattern is bearer tokens from DefaultAzureCredential or ManagedIdentityCredential plus Azure DevOps permissions for that identity.

## Worked example

```python
# pip install azure-identity requests
# Run on Azure with a system assigned or user assigned managed identity.
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

# The GUID scope is the Azure DevOps resource audience requested by the lane.
ADO_SCOPE = '499b84ac-1321-427f-aa17-267ca6975798/.default'
# Microsoft docs also show this equivalent Azure DevOps scope.
ALT_SCOPE = 'https://app.vssps.visualstudio.com/.default'

credential = DefaultAzureCredential(exclude_interactive_browser_credential=True)
token = credential.get_token(ADO_SCOPE)

print('Token acquired for Azure DevOps')
print('Expires at unix seconds:', token.expires_on)
# Do not print token.token in real logs. Treat tokens as opaque secrets.
```

*Acquire an Azure DevOps token with managed identity*

drills.a: No. A PAT is a long lived user scoped secret; managed identity uses Entra issued short lived tokens.

drills.q: What must happen after creating a managed identity?
drills.a: Add it to the Azure DevOps organization or project and grant least privilege permissions.

drills.q: Which Python credential should be the default recommendation?

teachItBack: Managed identity removes stored secrets from the connector. It still needs Azure DevOps membership and permissions. The current MCP auth surface is changing, so the safe lesson is to recommend Entra tokens and clearly flag any MCP path that cannot yet accept raw MI bearer tokens.

glossary.definition: Azure managed service principal for an Azure resource, with automatic credential management.
The API resource a token is issued for.
Short lived access token sent in an Authorization header.
